mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate profit visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer confidence (RSAFS) indicate weakening demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in demand contraction regimes show positive correlation with proxy declines (Rank IC > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side disruption, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days through elevated risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in supply shock scenarios exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with stagnant Fed funds rates creates stagflationary pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in stagflation regimes show positive correlation with proxy signals (Rank IC > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k during an expansion phase signals strong labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings outlook.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-payroll growth scenarios fall below 0.5% in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates conflicting demand signals, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through market uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines > 1% qoq and HOUST rises > 5%
failure_condition: 20d forward returns in demand divergence regimes show positive correlation with proxy signals (Rank IC > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in money supply growth (CPIAUCSL) during rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced liquidity.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in tightening regimes show positive correlation with proxy signals (Rank IC > 0.1).
